title: Spring Boot Microservices Architecture
summary: Contributed to decomposing a monolithic Java application into Spring Boot microservices with REST APIs.

content.value: Justdial's core backend was a large Java monolith that had diverged into a tangle of inter-dependencies. Deployments required the entire application to be released together, making it impossible to ship features independently across teams.

As part of a wider engineering initiative, contributed to decomposing the monolith into Spring Boot microservices following domain-driven design boundaries. Implemented REST API contracts using Spring MVC and OpenAPI specifications for each extracted service.

Each microservice was packaged as a Docker container and deployed via Jenkins pipelines. MySQL databases were separated by service with careful data migration strategies for services that shared tables in the original monolith.

Extracted 10+ services within a year. Teams owning individual services could now deploy 3× more frequently. The deployment risk of releasing one service no longer impacted the availability of unrelated services.
